title: Investment Memo Template
description: Defines the structure of a Nido Ventures investment memo. Each section maps to a primary call stage.

memo_version.description: Increments with each call (1 after Call 1, 2 after Call 2, 3 after Call 3)

section_definitions.title: Executive Summary
section_definitions.description: Deal terms, round structure, check size, valuation, and a concise overview of the opportunity. Should be readable as a standalone summary for the IC.

section_definitions.title: Investment Thesis
section_definitions.description: 3-5 bullet points on why this is a compelling opportunity for Nido. Covers market timing, founder-market fit, product differentiation, and growth potential.

section_definitions.title: Team & Founders
section_definitions.description: Founder backgrounds, relevant experience, founder-market fit, team composition, key hires needed, and advisory board.

section_definitions.title: Problem Statement
section_definitions.description: The industry pain point, status quo, who suffers, and the cost of inaction. Should clearly articulate why the problem matters and why now.

section_definitions.title: Product & Technology
section_definitions.description: What they've built, key features, tech stack, technical architecture, defensibility/moats, AI/ML usage, integrations, and product roadmap.

section_definitions.title: Business Model
section_definitions.description: Revenue model (SaaS, marketplace, transactional), pricing strategy, unit economics (CAC, LTV, gross margin, payback period), and expansion revenue dynamics.

section_definitions.title: Market Analysis
section_definitions.description: TAM/SAM/SOM with bottom-up sizing, market drivers, regulatory tailwinds/headwinds, and geographic considerations for LatAm.

section_definitions.title: GTM Strategy
section_definitions.description: Ideal customer profile with reasoning, sales motion, sales cycle, deal size, champion and decision-maker profiles, acquisition channels, and willingness-to-pay evidence.

section_definitions.title: Competitive Landscape
section_definitions.description: Direct and indirect competitors, differentiation, right to win, competitive positioning, win/loss analysis, and sustainable advantages.

section_definitions.title: Traction & Metrics
section_definitions.description: Client list, ARR/MRR, growth rate, pipeline, key KPIs (CAC, LTV, churn, NRR), and evidence of product-market fit.

section_definitions.title: Financial Review
section_definitions.description: Financial model assessment, revenue projections, burn rate, runway, path to profitability, and key assumptions.

section_definitions.title: Concerns & Challenges
section_definitions.description: Risks, red flags, open questions, and mitigation strategies. Accumulated across all three calls. Should be honest and actionable.

section_definitions.title: Scoring Rubric
section_definitions.description: Quantitative scoring (1-5) across dimensions: Team, Market, Product, Business Model, Traction, Competition. Includes rationale for each score and an overall recommendation.
